texto: Only he who can view his past as an abortion sprung from compulsion and need can use it to full advantage in the present. For what one has lived is at best comparable to a beautiful statue which has had all its limbs knocked off in transit, and now yields nothing but the precious block out of which the image of one's future must be hewn.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Walter Benajmin sobre sentido da existência e experiência vivida. A afirmação “Only he who can view his past as an abortion sprung from compulsion and need can use it to full a…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a motivacao, inspirational-attitude, reflexao, útil em momentos de inspiracao e reflexao.
